memoryArtifacts.description:
Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Treadwell, Treadway, Threadwell, Tredway, Threadway, Tredway, Tradewell, Tradway and many more.
First found in Suffolk where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: settlers who were recorded from the mid 17th century in the great migration from Europe. Migrants settled in the eastern seaboard from Newfoundland, to Maine, to Virginia, the Carolinas, and to the islands.
(From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Recorded in various spelling forms including Treadwell, Tredwell, Tretwell and even Tradewell, this interesting name is Medieval English. It is of occupational origins, and was found mainly in the Eastern and Midland counties. As such it may describe either a dresser of cloth as shown below, or possibly a builder and maintainer of watering holes called "waella". Originally these were natural springs probably walled (dressed) to allow for the easy collection of fresh water, but later the description was used for deep wells, as we know them. Where the origin related top the textile trade, the work of the dresser was to scour and thicken the raw cloth by beating and trampling it in water, hence the term to "tread well". There are a variety of modern surnames associated with these processes involved in the wool and cloth trade. They are usually regional. For instance the name Fuller being found mostly in South East England and East Anglia, whilst Tucker is recorded mainly in the South West, and the even more popular Walker in West and Northern England. Treadwell geographically seems to come in the middle and east of the country. Early examples of the surname recordings include: Thomas Treddewel of the county of Suffolk, where he appears in the register of members of the Abbey of Bec in 1248, and Gabriell Treadwell who married Hope Cordey on 12 July 1686, at All Hallows church, London Wall, in the city of London. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was sometimes known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ellen Tredwell於1568年出生在英國奧可福爾班波瑞時，她的父親Alexander Tredwell33歲，母親Dorothy Pomeroy33歲。她於1588年5月18日與Richard Alcock在英國奧可福爾艾普維爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1602年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯瓦爾可利福享年34歲。

nameMeanings.name: Tredwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Treadwell
meanings: English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English tred(de) ‘tread, walk, trample’ + wel(le) ‘well’, perhaps for a fuller or someone who trod grapes for wine production. Fulling was the process by which newly woven cloth was cleaned and shrunk by the use of heat, water, and pressure (from treading) before finally being stretched and laid out to dry on tenter hooks.

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Ellen Tredwell was born in 1568, in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, her father, Alexander Tredwell, was 33 and her mother, Dorothy Pomeroy, was 33. She married Richard Alcock on 18 May 1588, in Epwell, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1602, in Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ellen Tredwell est née en 1568, à Banbury, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Alexander Tredwell, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Pomeroy, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Alcock le 18 mai 1588, à Epwell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1602, à Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ellen Tredwell 1568 in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alexander Tredwell 33 und ihre Mutter Dorothy Pomeroy 33. Sie hat am 18. Mai 1588 in Epwell, Oxfordshire, England Richard Alcock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1602 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ellen Tredwell è nata il nel 1568 nel Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, Alexander Tredwell, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Dorothy Pomeroy, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Richard Alcock il 18 maggio 1588, nel Epwell, Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1602, nel Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 34 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen Tredwell(이)가 1568년 영국 스퍼드실르 반버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander Tredwell(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Pomeroy(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1588년 5월 18일 영국 스퍼드실르 프웰에서 Richard Alcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1602년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스월클리프에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ellen Tredwell nasceu em 1568, em Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alexander Tredwell, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Pomeroy, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Alcock em 18 de maio de 1588, em Epwell, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ellen Tredwell родилась в 1568 г. в местности банбури, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Alexander Tredwell было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Pomeroy, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Alcock 18 мая 1588 г. в местности эпвелл, оксфордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1602 г. в местности свалклифф, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ellen Tredwell nació en 1568, en Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alexander Tredwell, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Dorothy Pomeroy, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Richard Alcock el 18 de mayo de 1588, en Epwell, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1602, en Swalcliffe, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 34 años.
